commit.message:
Update ARL championship scouting standings

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix EGL 1.5 spec compliance issues

- C-1: eglChooseConfig/eglGetConfigs: configs=NULL returns count only
- C-2: eglChooseConfig: attrib_list=NULL treated as empty list
- C-3: EGL_TRANSPARENT_TYPE validation checks EGL_TRANSPARENT_RGB value
- C-4: eglDestroyContext: returns EGL_TRUE on success
- C-5: eglTerminate: returns EGL_TRUE on success; already-terminated is no-op
- C-6: Version comparison is now lexicographic (major, then minor)
- C-7: eglMakeCurrent: EGL_BAD_DISPLAY only set on failure
- C-8: eglMakeCurrent: result initialized to EGL_TRUE
- I-1: ES conformance bit selected based on requested ES version (1/2/3)
- I-2: eglCreateContext returns EGL_NO_CONTEXT on all error paths
- I-3: eglDestroySurface: returns EGL_TRUE on success
- I-4: eglGetConfigs: configs=NULL returns count only
- I-5: eglQueryContext EGL_CONTEXT_CLIENT_TYPE returns stored API type
- I-6: eglQueryContext EGL_CONTEXT_CLIENT_VERSION returns requested version
- I-7: eglSwapBuffers returns EGL_FALSE (not 0) on error
- I-8: eglTerminate: already-terminated display returns EGL_TRUE
- I-9: WGL_GENERIC_ACCELERATION_ARB maps to EGL_SLOW_CONFIG
- M-1: EGL_LEVEL allows negative values (overlay levels)
- M-4: eglCreatePlatformPixmapSurface uses EGL_BAD_NATIVE_PIXMAP error

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: security audit — input validation, injection prevention (#6)

* fix: security audit — input validation, injection prevention, tests

## Security fixes
- SQL injection: parameterized query in seed.rs has_table()
- LanceDB filter injection: ID format validation in delete/count_by_source/delete_batch
- Input validation on all endpoints: content length (64KiB), query length (2K),
  limit bounds (1-100), min_score clamping, source_type allowlist, visibility enum
- Delete endpoint validates ke-<uuid> ID format
- Prune endpoint validates max_age_days bounds (1-3650)

## Tests
- Added 7 new unit tests for validation logic (18 total, up from 11)

## Docs
- ADR-002: audit findings and fixes
- Copied ADR-040 from monorepo (vector knowledge store design)
- README: added Security section, fixed coverage threshold

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: resolve cargo-deny license and advisory failures

- Add 0BSD, CC0-1.0, BSL-1.0, NCSA to allowed licenses (permissive, from lancedb/fastembed transitive deps)
- Ignore RUSTSEC-2025-0119, RUSTSEC-2024-0436 (unmaintained advisories, no security impact)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix: Mobile streaming and UI issues

Fixes three UX issues:

1. Sections button wasn't functional - added click handler to toggle section visibility
2. Streaming disconnects on mobile tab switch - added page visibility event listener
3. Page refresh loses stream connection - added running scan detection on page load

New features:
- Sections button (📑) now toggles section visibility when clicked
- Page visibility API detects when user switches tabs (helps debugging)
- /api/scans/<repo_name> endpoint now returns running_experiment status
- On page load, checks if a scan is running and notifies user
- User can restart scan to reconnect if connection was lost

Improvements:
- Better mobile experience for long-running scans
- Clear messaging when scans are in progress after page reload
- Sections UI can now be toggled during scan results review

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix(integrations): Address review feedback

Per-integration commit/rollback for DB error
isolation.  Store poller task on app.state instead
of reaching into HAPoller._task.  Cancel the task
explicitly on shutdown to avoid hanging.  Improve
error isolation test to mock _process_integration
raising an exception.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Grimberg <tykeal@bardicgrove.org>

fix: restore NPM_TOKEN auth and add --access public for scoped package publish (#7475)

* fix: explicitly dispatch CI after tag push to build release binaries

GITHUB_TOKEN pushes do not trigger new workflow runs (GitHub prevents
recursive loop triggers). This means the tag push from the release
workflow never triggered osrm-backend.yml, so no binaries were built
or uploaded to the release assets.

Changes:
- Add workflow_dispatch trigger to osrm-backend.yml
- Add 'actions: write' permission to release workflow
- After pushing the tag, explicitly dispatch osrm-backend.yml on the
  tag ref; CI runs with GITHUB_REF=refs/tags/v* so PUBLISH=On and
  prebuilt binaries are built and uploaded
- Restore NODE_AUTH_TOKEN and add --access public for scoped npm package

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: remove redundant NPM_TOKEN and --access public from publish step

publishConfig.access=public is already set in package.json, so --access
public is redundant. OIDC via setup-node handles authentication when
id-token: write is set.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(cli): sanitize NaN/Infinity floats in JSON output for RFC 8259 compliance

- Add _sanitize_for_json() helper in cli.py: recursively replaces
  non-finite floats (nan, inf, -inf) with None before json.dumps()
- Apply sanitizer in _write_json_output() and _run_gate() JSON paths
- Replace float('inf') sentinel in _create_error_analysis() with 999.0
- Update avg_inflation filter to use status=='error' instead of inf check
- Root cause: _create_error_analysis() stored float('inf') which Python
  json.dumps() serializes as 'Infinity' (invalid per RFC 8259), causing
  jq parse error (exit code 5) in CI self-slop detection job

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: stabilize flaky Fluent UI Dialog portal tests

Replace userEvent.click() with fireEvent.click() for dialog-trigger
clicks in agentConfigCard, templateCard, and settingsPanel tests.

Root cause: userEvent simulates a full pointer/mouse/focus event
sequence that doesn't reliably trigger React state updates for
dialog-opening clicks under parallel test load in jsdom. The
multi-event simulation creates timing windows where the Portal
mount can be delayed past the findByRole timeout.

fireEvent.click() dispatches a single synchronous click event,
which deterministically triggers the onClick handler and React
state update. Dialog content interactions (clicking buttons within
the dialog) still use userEvent.click() since the portal is already
mounted by that point.

Also: use within(dialog).getByRole() to scope button queries to
the dialog portal, and configure asyncUtilTimeout for safety margin.

Verified: 20/20 consecutive full-suite runs (1835 tests each) pass.
Previously failed ~30% of runs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix: revert amidi timeout from 15s to 2s

amidi -t is a recording duration, not a deadline — setting it to 15s
caused every request to take the full 15 seconds to complete.

The BTS 15s timeout is a JavaScript async task watchdog (per-task
deadline in a queue), fundamentally different from amidi's -t flag
which controls how long the subprocess records incoming MIDI data.

2s gives adequate margin over typical device response time (~100-200ms)
while keeping load times fast.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Reset copied saved-solution pools when restarting subtree branch-and-bound, preserve tree status fields across copies, and harden saved-solution allocation, resize, and cleanup paths.

feat: support multiple OIDC issuers for mobile app tokens

The GT3 app uses a separate OIDC application (gt3-companion) on the same
Authentik instance. Its tokens have a different 'iss' claim than the
server's primary OIDC issuer (fluxhaus), causing JWT validation to fail
with 'unexpected iss claim value'.

Add OIDC_ADDITIONAL_ISSUERS env var (comma-separated) to accept tokens
from additional trusted issuers that share the same JWKS signing keys.

Also fix app's OIDC issuer URL: 'fluxhaus-server' → 'gt3-companion'.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: tap-to-play, auto-play, queue list, jumpTo + tests & docs

Interactions:
- TrackItem: entire row is now a <button> (reliable on iOS) — clicking
  anywhere plays the track; action buttons stop propagation
- PlayerContext: shouldAutoPlayRef triggers play() 100ms after load so
  tracks start immediately on selection (stays within iOS gesture context)
- PlayerView: upcoming queue renders below position counter using
  flex:1/min-height:0 so it fills available screen height; each row is
  a <button> that calls jumpTo(index) to skip to that track
- PlayerContext: expose jumpTo(index) — also sets shouldAutoPlayRef so
  the jumped-to track auto-plays
- Remove ResumeOverlay component and all needsResume/handleResume/
  dismissResume/visibilitychange logic — paused state is visible in player

CSS fixes:
- pv-meta, pv-progress-wrap, pv-controls get flex-shrink:0 so the queue
  list gets real remaining height instead of being compressed to zero

Tests (72 passing, up from 55):
- Add TrackItem.test.jsx — 8 tests: render, tap-to-play, stopPropagation
  on action buttons, playing indicator, thumbnail/placeholder
- Add PlayerContext reducer tests to reducer.test.js — 9 tests covering
  PLAY_QUEUE, SET_INDEX/jumpTo (including boundary clamping), SET_YT_STATE,
  and asserting needsResume is no longer part of state

Docs:
- README: updated features list, removed ResumeOverlay from structure,
  removed 'Tap to Resume' from limitations, updated test coverage table
- copilot-instructions.md: added Tests section — rules for adding,
  updating, and deleting tests alongside code changes

commit.message:
fix: don't push subminor version tag on scheduled builds (#64)

During scheduled (cron) runs github.ref_type=='branch' and
github.ref_name=='main' are both true, so the previous condition
  (github.ref_type == 'branch' && github.ref_name == 'main') || github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch'
incorrectly enabled the exact version tag (e.g. 2.0.8) on every
nightly build, re-pushing an immutable tag that should only be
created once.

Fix: key the enable flag on the event name instead.
- workflow_run  → new commit landed on main, push all tags including subminor
- workflow_dispatch → manual run, keep subminor for convenience
- schedule → nightly security-rebuild, push only :latest :major :major.minor and SHA

Co-authored-by: Copilot <copilot@github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add QuickDocumentCreation config and AI-based test assessment

- QuickDocumentCreation: when enabled, Researcher/PM/Architect produce
  minimal 1-paragraph docs using only project description + tech stack,
  cutting startup from ~15min to ~1min for testing
- Replace hardcoded TestableExtensions with AI-based testability assessment
  in TestEngineerAgent - model evaluates PR files and acceptance criteria
  to decide what tests are needed (unit/integration/UI)
- Set UITestsOnly=false now that AI assessment handles tier decisions
- Update type signatures from List<string> to IReadOnlyList<string> for
  consistency with GitHub API return types

commit.message:
Add emoji rating histogram with country flags to vote page (#32)

Adds a rating distribution histogram at the bottom of the vote page so
voters can see at a glance how they've spread their ratings across
songs.

- **New component `RatingHistogram`** — horizontal bar chart with one
row per emoji rating (🤩→🤮), proportional bar width, count/total with
percentage
- **Country flags per row** — each bar is followed by the flags of
countries that received that rating, making it easy to recall which
songs got which score
- **Rendered in `vote.tsx`** after the song list, only when songs exist;
data derived from the existing `voteRecord` and `songs` already
available server-side

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: valscion <482561+valscion@users.noreply.github.com>
